Rcode

library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
✔ purrr     1.1.0     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(rstan)
Loading required package: StanHeaders

rstan version 2.32.7 (Stan version 2.32.2)

For execution on a local, multicore CPU with excess RAM we recommend calling
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores()).
To avoid recompilation of unchanged Stan programs, we recommend calling
rstan_options(auto_write = TRUE)
For within-chain threading using `reduce_sum()` or `map_rect()` Stan functions,
change `threads_per_chain` option:
rstan_options(threads_per_chain = 1)


Attaching package: 'rstan'

The following object is masked from 'package:tidyr':

    extract
curlin <- read_csv("curling.csv")
Rows: 311 Columns: 6
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (1): CompetitionName
dbl (4): MatchID, EndID, USWin, Points
lgl (1): PowerPlay

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
#Potential data cleaning / Priors from Beijing
Beijing <- curlin %>% 
  filter(CompetitionName == "Beijing 2022 Olympic Winter Games")
mean(Beijing$Points) #3.22 average score of intentions
[1] 3.222222
Powerplay <- Beijing %>% filter(PowerPlay == T)
length(Beijing$PowerPlay) / 
  length(Powerplay$PowerPlay)  
[1] 8
# 0.125 Powerplays per year 
# (Dumb code and dumber user pp per year doesnt even matter)

curlin_excised <- curlin %>% 
  filter(!(CompetitionName %in% "Beijing 2022 Olympic Winter Games"))
# check how many observations we should have (32)
curlin %>% 
  filter(!str_detect(CompetitionName, "Beijing")) %>% 
  group_by(CompetitionName) %>% 
  summarize(num_matches = max(MatchID))
# A tibble: 3 × 2
  CompetitionName                               num_matches
  <chr>                                               <dbl>
1 World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship 2023          12
2 World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship 2024           9
3 World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship 2025          11
# create a new variable for successful shot 
# (deemed a Points score of higher than 2)
curlin <- curlin %>% mutate(Success = ifelse((Points > 2), 1, 0))

successful_powerplays <- curlin %>% 
  # filter to competitions other than Beijing Olympics, consider only powerplays
  filter(!str_detect(CompetitionName, "Beijing"),
         PowerPlay) %>% 
  # create a PowerPlaySuccessful variable
  mutate(PowerPlaySuccessful = if_else((Success == 1), TRUE, FALSE)) %>% 
  select(CompetitionName, MatchID, PowerPlaySuccessful)

n_successful_non_powerplays <- curlin %>% 
  # filter to competitions other than Beijing Olympics, 
  # consider only non powerplays
  filter(!str_detect(CompetitionName, "Beijing"),
         !PowerPlay) %>% 
  select(!c(PowerPlay, Points)) %>% 
  group_by(CompetitionName, MatchID) %>% 
  # count how many successful non powerplays there were per match
  summarize(SuccessfulNonPowerPlays = sum(Success))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'CompetitionName'. You can override using
the `.groups` argument.
final_curling <- curlin %>% 
  # filter to competitions other than Beijing Olympics
  filter(!str_detect(CompetitionName, "Beijing")) %>%
  select(!Success) %>% 
  # pivot so there's one row per match. 
  # information about powerplay and points go to a column per end
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = "EndID",
    values_from = c("PowerPlay", "Points"),
    values_fill = list("PowerPlay" = FALSE, "Points" = 0)
  ) %>% 
  # determine if there's at least one powerplay in a match
  mutate(
    PowerPlayPresent = 
      if_else((PowerPlay_1 | PowerPlay_2 | PowerPlay_3 | PowerPlay_4 | 
                 PowerPlay_5 | PowerPlay_6 | PowerPlay_7 | PowerPlay_8 | 
                 PowerPlay_9), TRUE, FALSE)
  ) %>% 
  # add on information regarding successful powerplays
  left_join(successful_powerplays, 
            by = join_by("CompetitionName" == "CompetitionName", 
                         "MatchID" == "MatchID")) %>% 
  # add on information regarding number of successful non powerplays
  left_join(n_successful_non_powerplays, 
            by = join_by("CompetitionName" == "CompetitionName", 
                         "MatchID" == "MatchID")) %>% 
  # turn NA values from lack of powerplay in match to FALSE, 
  # create index for competition name
  mutate(PowerPlaySuccessful = 
           if_else(is.na(PowerPlaySuccessful), FALSE, PowerPlaySuccessful),
         CompetitionIndex = case_when(
           CompetitionName == "World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship 2023" ~ 1,
           CompetitionName == "World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship 2024" ~ 2,
           CompetitionName == "World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship 2025" ~ 3
         ))
final_curling %>% 
  summarize_at(vars(starts_with("PowerPlay_")), mean)
# A tibble: 1 × 9
  PowerPlay_1 PowerPlay_2 PowerPlay_3 PowerPlay_4 PowerPlay_5 PowerPlay_6
        <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>
1           0           0           0           0      0.0625       0.188
# ℹ 3 more variables: PowerPlay_7 <dbl>, PowerPlay_8 <dbl>, PowerPlay_9 <dbl>
successful_powerplays_beijing <- curlin %>% 
  # filter to competitions other than Beijing Olympics, consider only powerplays
  filter(str_detect(CompetitionName, "Beijing"),
         PowerPlay) %>% 
  # create a PowerPlaySuccessful variable
  mutate(PowerPlaySuccessful = if_else((Success == 1), TRUE, FALSE)) %>% 
  select(CompetitionName, MatchID, PowerPlaySuccessful)

n_successful_non_powerplays_beijing <- curlin %>% 
  # filter to competitions other than Beijing Olympics, 
  # consider only non powerplays
  filter(str_detect(CompetitionName, "Beijing"),
         !PowerPlay) %>% 
  select(!c(PowerPlay, Points)) %>% 
  group_by(CompetitionName, MatchID) %>% 
  # count how many successful non powerplays there were per match
  summarize(SuccessfulNonPowerPlays = sum(Success))
`summarise()` has grouped output by 'CompetitionName'. You can override using
the `.groups` argument.
final_curling_beijing <- curlin %>% 
  # filter to competitions other than Beijing Olympics
  filter(str_detect(CompetitionName, "Beijing")) %>%
  select(!Success) %>% 
  # pivot so there's one row per match. 
  # information about powerplay and points go to a column per end
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = "EndID",
    values_from = c("PowerPlay", "Points"),
    values_fill = list("PowerPlay" = FALSE, "Points" = 0)
  ) %>% 
  # determine if there's at least one powerplay in a match
  mutate(
    PowerPlayPresent = if_else((PowerPlay_1 | PowerPlay_2 | PowerPlay_3 | 
                                  PowerPlay_4 | PowerPlay_5 | PowerPlay_6 | 
                                  PowerPlay_7 | PowerPlay_8 | PowerPlay_9), 
                               TRUE, FALSE)
  ) %>% 
  # add on information regarding successful powerplays
  left_join(successful_powerplays_beijing, 
            by = join_by("CompetitionName" == "CompetitionName", 
                         "MatchID" == "MatchID")) %>% 
  # add on information regarding number of successful non powerplays
  left_join(n_successful_non_powerplays_beijing, 
            by = join_by("CompetitionName" == "CompetitionName", 
                         "MatchID" == "MatchID")) %>% 
  # turn NA values from lack of powerplay in match to FALSE, create index for competition name
  mutate(PowerPlaySuccessful = 
           if_else(is.na(PowerPlaySuccessful), FALSE, PowerPlaySuccessful),
         CompetitionIndex = case_when(
           CompetitionName == "World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship 2023" ~ 1,
           CompetitionName == "World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship 2024" ~ 2,
           CompetitionName == "World Mixed Doubles Curling Championship 2025" ~ 3
         ))
final_curling_beijing %>% 
  group_by(PowerPlayPresent) %>% 
  summarize(win_percentage = mean(USWin))
# A tibble: 1 × 2
  PowerPlayPresent win_percentage
  <lgl>                     <dbl>
1 TRUE                      0.333

Partial Pooling for Q1 (DO NOT USE):

q1_prior <- glm(USWin ~ PowerPlay_5 + PowerPlay_6 + PowerPlay_7 + PowerPlay_8, 
                data = final_curling_beijing, family = "binomial")

q1_prior %>% 
  summary()

Call:
glm(formula = USWin ~ PowerPlay_5 + PowerPlay_6 + PowerPlay_7 + 
    PowerPlay_8, family = "binomial", data = final_curling_beijing)

Coefficients: (1 not defined because of singularities)
                  Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
(Intercept)     -1.957e+01  1.075e+04  -0.002    0.999
PowerPlay_5TRUE -3.017e-08  1.521e+04   0.000    1.000
PowerPlay_6TRUE -3.016e-08  1.521e+04   0.000    1.000
PowerPlay_7TRUE  1.957e+01  1.075e+04   0.002    0.999
PowerPlay_8TRUE         NA         NA      NA       NA

(Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)

    Null deviance: 11.4573  on 8  degrees of freedom
Residual deviance:  8.3178  on 5  degrees of freedom
AIC: 16.318

Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 18
ppq_1_data <- list(N = length(final_curling$CompetitionName), # number of matches
                   k = length(unique(final_curling$CompetitionName)), # Number of competitions
                   PowerPlay_5 = final_curling$PowerPlay_5,
                   PowerPlay_6 = final_curling$PowerPlay_6,
                   PowerPlay_7 = final_curling$PowerPlay_7,
                   PowerPlay_8 = final_curling$PowerPlay_8,
                   y = final_curling$USWin,
                   i = final_curling$CompetitionIndex, # for indexings sake
                   u0 = 0,
                   u1 = 0,
                   u2 = 0,
                   u3 = 0,
                   u4 = 0,
                   v0 = 1,
                   v1 = 1,
                   v2 = 1,
                   v3 = 1,
                   v4 = 1,
                   alpha0 = 1,
                   alpha1 = 1,
                   alpha2 = 1,
                   alpha3 = 1,
                   alpha4 = 1,
                   eta0 = 1,
                   eta1 = 1,
                   eta2 = 1,
                   eta3 = 1,
                   eta4 = 1
)


set.seed(454)

fit <- stan(file = "partial_pooling_q1.stan", 
            data = ppq_1_data, 
            chains = 4, 
            warmup = 1000,
            iter = 2000,
            control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999))

Partial Pooling #1 Version 2 (THIS WAS USED)

ppq_1_data_v2 <- list(N = length(final_curling$CompetitionName), # number of matches
                   k = length(unique(final_curling$CompetitionName)), # Number of competitions
                   PowerPlayPresent = final_curling$PowerPlayPresent,
                   y = final_curling$USWin,
                   i = final_curling$CompetitionIndex, # for indexings sake
                   u0 = -0.30,
                   u1 = 0,
                   v0 = 1,
                   v1 = 1,
                   alpha0 = 1,
                   alpha1 = 1,
                   eta0 = 1,
                   eta1 = 1
)


set.seed(355)

fit_q1 <- stan(file = "partial_pooling_q1_v2.stan", 
            data = ppq_1_data_v2, 
            chains = 4, 
            warmup = 1000,
            iter = 8000,
            control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999,
                           max_treedepth = 16))

bulk_ess_beta0 <- vector(mode = "double", length = 3)
bulk_ess_beta1 <- vector(mode = "double", length = 3)

for (i in 1:3) {
  bulk_ess_beta0[i] <- ess_bulk(extract(fit_q1, 
                                        pars = "beta0", permuted = FALSE)[,,i])
  bulk_ess_beta1[i] <- ess_bulk(extract(fit_q1, 
                                        pars = "beta1", permuted = FALSE)[,,i])
}

rhat_beta0 <- vector(mode = "double", length = 3)
rhat_beta1 <- vector(mode = "double", length = 3)

for (i in 1:3) {
  rhat_beta0[i] <- Rhat(extract(fit_q1, pars = "beta0", 
                                permuted = FALSE)[,,i])
  rhat_beta1[i] <- Rhat(extract(fit_q1, pars = "beta1", 
                                permuted = FALSE)[,,i])
}



traceplot(fit_q1, pars = "beta0")
traceplot(fit_q1, pars = "beta1")
print(fit_q1, pars = c("beta0", "beta1"))

post-warmup draws per chain=7000, total post-warmup draws=28000.

Samples were drawn using NUTS(diag_e) at Thu Apr  9 20:09:12 2026.
For each parameter, n_eff is a crude measure of effective sample size,
and Rhat is the potential scale reduction factor on split chains (at 
convergence, Rhat=1).
print(fit_q1, pars = c("exp_beta0", "exp_beta1"))

For each parameter, n_eff is a crude measure of effective sample size,
and Rhat is the potential scale reduction factor on split chains (at 
convergence, Rhat=1).

Partial Pooling for Q2.

Do these results (Odds of winning a curling match) vary by whether or not the play (power or otherwise) “failed in its intended result”? Beta hyperparameters need to be done properly $$ Y_{ij} | _{ij},_0, _1, 2 &Bernoulli({ij}) \ () &= _0 + _1 PowerPlay + 2 Success \ {ij} &= invlogit(_0 + 1 PowerPlay{ij} + 2 Success{ij}) \ B_0 &N(m_0, s_0^2) \ B_1 &N(m_1, s_1^2) \ B_2 &N(m_2, s_2^2) \

m_0 & (u_0, v_0^2) \ m_1 & (u_1, v_1^2) \ m_2 & (u_2, v_2^2) \

1 / s_0^2 & (_0, _0) \ 1 / s_1^2 & (_1, _1) \ 1 / s_2^2 & (_2, _2) \

$$

# 
#   int<lower=1> N; // total number of observations - sets
#   int<lower=1> k; // number of matches
#   vector[N] x; // this is powerplay
#   vector[N] x2; // this is success
#   int<lower=0, upper=1> y[N];
#   int<lower=0> i[N]; // these are our indices for each Match
#                       // We need right here our two indicator variables
#   
#   // hyperparameters for regression coefficients (Betas) and likelihood variance
#   real u0;
#   real u1;
#   real u2;
#   real<lower=0> v0;
#   real<lower=0> v1;
#   real<lower=0> v2;
# 
#   
#   // hyperparameters for random effects (Gamma funcs)
#   real<lower=0> alpha0;
#   real<lower=0> alpha1;
#   real<lower=0> alpha2;
# 
#   real<lower=0> eta0;
#   real<lower=0> eta1;
#   real<lower=0> eta2;

# curlin <- curlin %>% mutate(Points = ifelse((Points > 2), 1, 0))

PPQ2Data <- list(x = curlin$PowerPlay,
                 x2 = curlin$Points,
                 y = curlin$USWin,
                 N = length(curlin$CompetitionName), #sets
                 k = length(unique(curlin$MatchID)),# Number of matches
                 i = curlin$MatchID, # for indexings sake
                 u0 = 1,
                 u1 = 1,
                 u2 = 1,
                 
                 v0 = 1,
                 v1 = 1,
                 v2 = 1,
                 
                 alpha0 = 1,
                 alpha1 = 1,
                 alpha2 = 1,
                 
                 eta0 = 1,
                 eta1 = 1,
                 eta2 = 1
                 
)

fit <- stan(file = "PPQ2.stan", 
            data = PPQ2Data, 
            chains = 4, 
            warmup = 1000,
            iter = 2000)
Trying to compile a simple C file
Running /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Resources/bin/R CMD SHLIB foo.c
using C compiler: ‘Apple clang version 17.0.0 (clang-1700.6.3.2)’
using SDK: ‘MacOSX26.2.sdk’

Samples were drawn using NUTS(diag_e) at Thu Apr  9 20:09:54 2026.
For each parameter, n_eff is a crude measure of effective sample size,
and Rhat is the potential scale reduction factor on split chains (at 
convergence, Rhat=1).
traceplot(fit)
'pars' not specified. Showing first 10 parameters by default.
intercepts <- summary(fit, pars = c('beta0'))$summary[,1]
slopes <- summary(fit, pars = c('beta1'))$summary[,1]
slopes2 <- summary(fit, pars = c('beta2'))$summary[,1]

slopes[1] + intercepts[1] + slopes2[1]# why si this bigger than 1
beta1[1] 
2.201019 
mean(intercepts)
[1] 1.675605
mean(slopes)
[1] -0.07722404
  curlin %>%   ggplot(aes(PowerPlay, USWin)) +
  geom_point(col = "white") + 
  theme_classic() +
  geom_abline(slope = slopes, intercept = intercepts, col = "grey")
